text.title: JAPAN GIRLS TRIP ITINERARY🍡 ₊˚⊹ (matcha cafes, shibuya crossing, shinkansen train, street food)
text.u: [Music]
Good morning from Tokyo.
>> Yeah, that's right. I'm here. I'm here.
And what did it take for us to get here?
Let me tell you.
[Music]
Guys, this is going to be really hard
for me to say. My first Asia vlog is in
Tokyo, but this is actually our third
destination of the trip. I'm sorry for
being dishonest. We actually started in
Hong Kong, but I haven't posted about
that yet because we had to leave 12
hours in.
>> Typhoon Vgasa is currently hitting Hong
Kong right now.
>> Suspending flights in and out for 36
hours. That means at least 700 flights
will be impacted, will be cancelled. It
could be the strongest storm since 2018.
>> Um,
>> your one scrunchie on the table.
>> I took it off cuz it looked ugly.
>> Okay.
>> Um, yeah. So, we started in Hong Kong.
We were supposed to stay there for a few
days, but then disaster struck.
Yeah. Yeah. So, that happened pretty
much right after we landed. So, we had
to get the f out of there. So, we went
to Osaka and then I did not vlog there.
Ashley vlogged there. So, you can go
watch it there. Go watch her video. And
now we are in Tokyo. Let me show you
around. Okay. I should just point it
this way actually.
>> Freaking jump.
>> Okay. I'm trying.
>> You literally like do like a horse
gallop, bro. Stop.
>> You like glided like 10 ft.
>> Stop it. So, we're staying here in
Shabuya and we're here for 24 hours. We
only have one day here. So, we're going
to see what we get into. I want to see
if I can flip upside down in this like I
used to at home.
>> Oh, I did it.
>> Okay. So,
>> here's the agenda for today.
>> It's actually so [ __ ] funny.
>> What's up, guys? We're in Tokyo. Spend
24 hours with me in Tokyo. I'm just
kidding.
So, here's what's on the agenda today.
So, I'm going to start off the day with
some ramen. We're going to go to
Shabuya. We're going to look at Shabuya
Crossing. We're going to look at the
statue of that dog. We're going to go to
Harajuku. We're going to go to Asaka.
We're going to go to Ginsa, I think. I
don't know. We have a full day planned.
I can't breathe anymore. The way that my
lungs are bending inside of here is
making it so that I cannot breathe that
well. So, how about we get started with
the day? Let's head out.
[Music]
So, what's going on right now, Queen?
>> We're at vending machine ramen.
>> I got this and mom got this with a spicy
broth.
>> Yeah.
>> So, good. This meal is so different to
me. Like I was expecting like an instant
meal.
>> Yeah, I know, right?
>> Meat day
>> almost. It was way too much food, but it
was so freaking good. And I feel really
bad. I wish Pono or Andrew was here to
finish the bowl for me. There's no man
here to finish my food. That's all
they're good for.
Mega Donkey Hotel. This is the biggest
donkeyote in Japan, I'm pretty sure. And
my mom is so excited.
>> Cute.
>> I know. Look at this cute grocery.
>> Yeah, I don't carry it today.
>> Oh, mom's just getting it.
>> Mom literally just bought the first
thing she saw.
>> We might be buying a lot.
>> Okay, mom's going crazy already.
>> Overwhelmed right now. Look at flavors.
>> Oh my god.
>> Wow.
>> Okay.
>> Okay, let's go.
Wasabi
[Music]
flavor.
>> Got it.
>> That's crazy. I love it.
>> I like this guy.
>> We're going to get you out of there,
buddy. Just hang in there. This is like
in Toy Story with Buzz. Blueberry
cheesecake flavor and it looks like
this.
>> Oh my god. Wow. Mountain Fuji looks so
old.
>> Like that's actually fine. Wait, I feel
like this should be like ice flavored.
>> Yeah,
>> like minty gum.
>> Oh my god.
>> Purple sweet potato.
>> Wait, sake flavor.
>> What the
[Music]
She's literally running.
>> Oh, Amber.
>> Amber. No. Uhoh.
>> We're going to get by the cars. We're
going to go see Hatiko, the dog that his
owner died and he waited by the train
forever and ever. So, this is his
statue.
[Music]
[Music]
Stay strong, girls. Stick together.
[Music]
[Laughter]
Yes.
>> Oh, yeah. Very nice.
>> Are you from
>> Oh, yeah. We're on
>> New York.
>> Japan.
>> Good. Good.
5% for your tissue paper.
>> Oh, wow. Oh, it's literally just tissue.
That's actually really nice. That was
not I thought he was trying to sell us
something. Period. We got a little mist
that's supposed to help drop your body
temperature cuz it's so hot here.
Oh. Oh, it's minty. Smells good. It's
pretty good.
[Music]
[Music]
[Music]
Heat. Heat.
[Music]
[Music]
[Music]
I'm getting busy.
[Music]
Now you have
>> perfect.
[Music]
It's okay.
I just want to be sure.
[Music]
It's gone. I forgot. We're in Actually,
where we at? We are in Asaka. This is
one of my most favorite places in Tokyo.
This is where I am bringing the family.
We're gonna go to Camaron
G. Camino.
>> And for dinner, we're kind of thinking
of getting pizza because some of our
friends have said that even if they're
from New York, the best pizza they've
ever had in their lives is from Tokyo.
Yeah. There's a place called Seavoy
that's supposed to be super good, but I
don't know if we'll be able to get in
cuz we don't have a reservation, but
we'll try. Yeah. Yeah. So, I'm kind of
curious. I mean, honestly, I don't
really want to eat pizza in Tokyo, but
the fact that they said that is kind of
something that needs to be fact checked.
And we're from New York, so we'll let
you know the truth.
>> We're Italian.
>> Not only are we from New York, but we
are also Italian. Just kidding. We're
not. But we do have Italian cousins.
>> Wow.
Very,
>> wait until we get outside. It's
literally going to bring even more
things. M. Let's do it. Here we are.
It's beautiful. Oh my goodness. I
thought the food stalls would be open
and nothing is open. So, that's really
awkward. But we can go walk through it
anyways. It'll be really pretty. Okay.
Just imagine that there's a lot of
delicious food here. Wow. It's bustling.
>> It's beautiful. Oh my goodness. The
sights and smells.
[Music]
S Soji temple.
[Music]
We got this. This, this, and this. And
we're going to get another one of these.
Another one. Margarita and marinara.
[Music]
>> It tastes like there's butter on it.
That's really good. But is it better
than New York pizza?
Never mind that. But is really good.
Just finished that pizza. It was very
good. And they were so nice in there.
But people said that was the best pizza
in the world. Come on, bro. You cannot
fool me.
>> I thought it was mid.
>> I thought it was like perfectly fine.
What was that weird pizza propaganda
that we fell for? Why would we think
that the best pizza in the world would
be in Tokyo? We should have known
better.
>> For me, it was a skip. It's all right. I
just can't believe I could have gotten
freaking Wagu or Katu tonight and
instead we ate some floppy Neapolitan
pizza. What the heck was that? Don't
mess with me. Originally, I wanted to
get something after dinner, but now I'm
kind of too full. So, I think we're
going to get some snacks from the
7-Eleven. Oh, huh?
>> Wait, where?
>> Across the street. Wait here. 7-Eleven.
Oh, there it is.
>> On the glasses.
>> I just need something Japanese.
Stop.
>> Why did I fall for it? Why did we fall
for it?
>> Now, this is where you should really
allocate your belly space.
[Music]
Sad is I'm actually really full. I'm
just so I need to redeem myself so bad
that I'm going to eat anyways.
[Music]
Come back in the window. Oh my god, he's
so cute.
Hi, buddy. We're really pissing him off.
He's so mad. We're back at home and I
have a little 7-Eleven haul. I decided
to get some souvenirs for people.
Starting off with these rice crackers.
This is almost the same thing. Some
grape gummies. Ashley got her classic
grape jelly and tofu bar. I think my mom
got this. It's a little soy squid. And
she also got these little corn puff
bars. They feel like they have the
texture of a puffy Cheeto. And then
finally, I got a little steamed
cheesecake. These are so good. So that's
all for tonight. And then tomorrow
morning, we have to take the Shinkansen,
the bullet train down to Kyoto. So we'll
see you guys there. But for now, I'm
going to bed.
[Music]
Good morning. Good morning.
[Music]
Heat. Heat.
[Music]
Hey, what's up, guys? We are in Kyoto
now. I know this is a little Tokyo vlog,
but you're getting a taste of Kyoto.
Enjoy it. Consider it a gift. Um, we're
now getting katucare or katu curry. Uh,
it's like 400 p.m. now. We accidentally
have not eaten yet because we had to
take the train down here and there was a
lot of mishaps where we were running
late and just scrambling. So, there was
no breakfast, no lunch. We're straight
to dinner. Um, we're getting a little
bit hungry. You must be John the way
that I'm hanging. Hangry. That's good. I
said that to Ashley earlier and now I'm
trying to save.
>> I'm literally crying laughing. That was
iconic. You must be John. The way that
I'm angry
angry. We're at Coco something.
>> Cocoa. So good. Oh no. I'm eating this
one. Oh my god. So delicious.
>> Let's do a transition to one of the
foods here.
Delicious curry. This is mine. And this
is our mom. She got squid and coat. And
I got scrambled egg and kataku. I got
veggie and I got corn soup. And the corn
soup is literally so good.
[Music]
It is. I'm so hungry.
>> Hey, Ashley, you want to get more pizza
over here? You love the pizza in Japan.
You're obsessed with getting pizza here.
is perfect. Get roasted. Get roasted.
Get roasted. Get roasted. Get roasted.
Get roasted. Get roasted. Get roasted.
Get roasted. Get roasted. Get roasted.
Boom. Where are those? Where are those?
Where are those? Okay. Damn Daniel. Damn
Daniel. Where are those?
>> Mom said be quiet because you're wrong.
>> Whoa. Mom loves the pea stream.
>> Girls,
>> you know, Amber and mom.
>> Cute.
>> Beautiful willow trees. So nice.
>> Amber looks cool. Yeah, you look
beautiful.
>> I keep seeing this ad and it's killing
me. Why did they add so many layers to
her? Like lipstick, whiskers, lipstick,
whiskers, eyeshadow, glasses, a scar.
Like there's just simply too many damn
accessories on this woman.
[Music]
Grape mochi dauku. Okay, there should be
a huge grape inside of this mochi. It's
crowning.
>> Let's eat it. Cheers.
>> Wo. I did not anticipate it's all filled
with white bean, but it's pretty good.
Um, except that the grape is literally
so tiny. Dang it.
>> Oh, man.
>> Thought that this whole thing was going
to be a grape.
>> I know.
>> Maker. This is pretty good.
>> It's pretty good.
>> For the sake of the video, I think the
veil is better if I say it's good. It's
pretty good.
>> Yeah,
>> it actually is good though.
Good evening, everyone. Um, we just
dropped off our mom at the Airbnb cuz
she is so sleepy. And the girls are
going out on the town.
>> We're going to do some shopping for our
hubbies and for our little babies. They
need their Japanese fashion
>> and for Andrew. The hubbies and the
habibis.
>> The habibis. All the habibises. Andrew
is really adamant of like, can you
please, please, please go to the store?
So, we're going to
>> and it's going to be awesome.
[Music]
[Music]
Matcha candy apple from the infamous
candy apple in Kyoto.
>> I've never heard of this, but Ashley
says she's seen it on TikTok, and
apparently they use a really good apple.
>> Mhm.
>> This looks a little bit like it came out
of Shrek Swamp, but let's try it.
>> Andrew is so jealous right now. Andrew
loves candy apples. Insert the photo of
him holding that candy apple as a child.
>> Yeah, the apple is really good, but I
think I would have preferred it without
the matcha. Oh,
>> is the matcha kind of overpowering?
>> Like a little random in it.
>> It tastes like culinary grade matcha is
the thing.
>> No.
>> Oh, it probably is.
>> Oh,
>> right.
>> The apple itself is really good. You
know, I still like the little hint of
matcha. I like it.
>> Okay. Well, I'm glad you do. Cavity
central.
>> Remember when we did skateboard karaoke
with Tono in there?
>> This is crazy.
Say you bring it right back. Come on.
>> Good times. The best night of our life.
These look like freaking taxiderermy.
He's so guilty. He looks like he just
ate the last chicken nugget. Mini chair.
Heat.
[Music]
Heat.
[Music]
I want boba. Dang it. Oh, all time.
We're going to show you our clothes and
our snacks that we got. First of all, we
went to Capital. This is one of Andrew's
favorite stores, and he asked us to come
here. So, we got him this blue shirt
with a pocket. And then I got Pono this
little shirt. So cute. Which I also got
for Andrew at the last capital store we
visited, but in a different color. So
they will be in fact matching. Amber and
I got a scarf to share because that's
what we do. It looks like this. It has
like their classic capital print socks
to match. And then Ashley got these very
beautiful lacy socks for our friend.
Okay. Then we went to Second Street. And
basically, let me show you exactly what
we got. This is very amazing. Ashley
found this Stuy shirt and she was like,
"Oh, we should get it for one of the
boys."
>> Don't say that cuz then Andrew is going
to watch this and be really pissed off.
I think she was thinking of giving it to
Andrew and then I said, "Wait, what if
we gave it to Pono?" So now it's for
Pono.
>> Andrew's going to see this and be really
mad.
>> He's going to be like, "What the fuck?"
I found this skirt. This was like $10 or
$15 and it's just like a really cute
pleated skirt. Next, I found the most
amazing piece for my mom. I found a
Miyaki shirt and guys, it was very
gorgeous. It was around $100 USD, which
is kind of expensive, but actually for
this piece, it was a great price and it
was so pretty and the most perfect
color. Here's what it looked like when I
tried it on. So gorgeous. Then we got
all of these.
This is crazy cuz I also want to do a
video that's like only eating at
convenience stores for 24 hours, but
I've basically been doing that challenge
every day since we've been here.
Anyways, like we're going so Bro, you
can't even see on like we have infinite
items over here. These sweet potato
chips I ate on the train earlier and
they were so good. I had to buy three
more. Mom got her dried squid. I got two
of these SPF sprays as souvenirs for my
friends. They're really good. These
chocolate covered grape jellies are also
for friends. These things are crazy.
They're like MSG crackers. That's what
we call them. 250% MSG flavoring. Oh my
god, bro. You put this on your tongue,
it goes crazy.
>> And I got this. So delicious.
You want to do these, but those are just
like kind of bacon crackers. And then I
wanted to try these things that mom got,
except they turned into powder dust on
the train.
>> Are they supposed to be sticks? Oh, this
one smells like a delicious barbecue
cheesy poof. Well, this one has a corn
picture on it.
>> Cheers. Cheers.
>> Oh my god, mine is so good.
>> Mine is so good, guys. Mine is so good.
Wait, this is something I like feel like
I ate as a child. It's like the perfect
barbecue chip flavor. And the corn
flavor is immaculate, guys. I've been
obsessed with everything that's corn
flavored.
>> This is crazy cuz this was like
literally 5 cents, I think, to get one
of these.
>> M. I think mine's better.
>> No.
>> Mhm.
>> Here, you tried the corn, too.
>> M. Okay.
>> I think they're good.
>> Mhm.
>> Next, I really want you to try these cuz
there's literally five chips total in
this bag, but they're so good. And also,
like, they kind of have that really
organic vibe where you have to like chew
it for a bit, but then it turns into the
most delicious flavor in your mouth.
It's like a creamy sweet potato. Amber
made us go on the hunt for these cuz
they're only available at like specific
7-Elevens. Cheers. Cheers.
M. No, that's really good. It tastes
like they didn't add any sweetener. So,
it's just like full natural flavor of
the potato. Sticks to your teeth, but
like in a good way. I just love these
Japanese sweet potatoes. So, to eat them
in chip form, it's so good. Mom, you
want Japanese sweet potato chip? I think
actually we should give these a shot cuz
they have a secret spice blend that
we've never had before. This is coated
in flavor.
It's so thick.
>> It's so coated. I don't even know if I
want this be.
>> Wait, I'll switch with you, bro. I want
that one.
>> Yay. Okay, let's switch.
>> Cheers.
>> Cheers.
>> Damn, these chips are so good.
>> My layer of seasoning was literally way
too thick.
>> Well, for me, cuz this is 250%.
>> M. It actually tastes like black pepper
to me. I think maybe it's black pepper.
I really like it.
>> Mhm.
Oh my god, it's so good and like so
sweet and so savory. Give this to a
Victorian child. It will blow their
effing mind. I think I still prefer the
original though. I like this one. But I
love you. And what's crazy is that we
keep going to the 7-Elevens and eating
so much stuff from there that I'm
starting to get sick of it. But I'm
going to keep doing it. I'm going to go
back tomorrow and buy even more stuff.
Well, this was a beautiful and amazing
day. And if you want to see Amber eat
more food from 7-Eleven, stay tuned.
Stay tuned. That is Foli coming. I act
like I don't want to do it, but you know
I do. I act like I'm sick of the egg
salad sandwiches, but you know I'm going
to eat another one. Well, I think that's
where this vlog ends. Thanks for
following us from Tokyo to Kyoto. I'm so
excited to show you guys the rest of our
trip because this is just the beginning.
We got Japan, Korea, and Hong Kong on
the agenda. So stay tuned and we'll see
you guys later. Bye. Beautiful twin
sisters out.
[Music]
